description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

**SPIKE** (time-boxed: half-day). Decide the disposition of test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs Scenario 7 (`:ecto_user_scenario`), which is currently `@tag :skip` with a fresh note from `.13` ("cover-instrumentation issue unrelated to this tickets Code.Server-lock fix").

Closes:
- **F12** (HIGH, second half — `:literal_floor_scenario` already un-skipped by `.16`)

The scenario gates the Spike-I bytecode-identical-restore invariant against macro-heavy DSLs (Ecto, Phoenix). Without it, the project ships v1 with no green coverage on its most load-bearing claim.

## Spike output

This is an investigation ticket, not a fix ticket. Half-day time box. Output is one of:

**Option A — fold into `.29` (F23 combine baseline+coverage instrumented run):**
The root cause is `:cover.compile_beam` not producing bytecode-identical output for Ecto-style schema macros. Document the deferral target; close `.19` as decided. The un-skip happens in `.29`.

**Option B — open `.19b` as a concrete fix ticket:**
The root cause is standalone (e.g. ExUnit setup ordering, fixture pollution, an EctoUser-specific macro that needs special handling). Open `.19b` with a focused fix plan; un-skip during `.19b` implementation.

**Option C — write a non-:cover parallel scenario:**
Exercise bytecode-identity restore against a macro-heavy module without going through :cover. Document the :cover + Ecto interaction as a known limitation but unblock the Spike-I evidence at .19s scope. ~2 hours work.

Any output updates README "Known caveats" with the actual root cause and a concrete deferral target — not the current vague "cover-instrumentation issue unrelated."

## Verification

Investigation completes with one of A/B/C decided. Decision recorded as a comment on this ticket. README caveats updated. `.19b` opened if Option B.

## Allowed touches

- README.md, CHANGELOG.md (caveat language)
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs (only if Option C; minimal scope)
- New scratch directory under priv/helper_scripts/ if needed for investigation

## Out of Scope

- Fixing the `:cover` + Ecto interaction in lib/ source — that lives in `.19b` (if Option B) or `.29` (if Option A)
- Refactoring the e2e suite beyond un-skipping if Option C

## Time budget

Hard cap: 4 hours. If no clear root cause emerges in 4 hours, default to Option A (fold into `.29`) and document the unknown as a deferred investigation. Set a timer.

## Merge gate

- [ ] Investigation conclusion recorded as a ticket comment
- [ ] README updated with root cause + deferral target
- [ ] `.19b` opened if Option B; otherwise this ticket is the terminal node
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

comments.text:
SPIKE DECISION: Option B
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-ecto-spike/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

## Root cause (clean, well within the 4h time box)

Scenario 7's `baseline.failed: 1, passed: 5` is NOT a `:cover` +
Ecto-style-DSL interaction. The macro-injected callbacks
(`__schema_kind__/0`, `field/2`-generated `name/0` / `age/0`,
`birthday/1`, the persisted `:lane_schema_kind` attribute) all survive
`:cover.compile_beam/1` → `:cover.stop/0` → `:code.purge/1` →
`:code.load_file/1` byte-for-byte. The module's `__info__(:md5)`
returns to its disk-baseline value after `:cover.stop/0`. There is no
cover-instrumentation issue with this fixture.

The actual root cause is a fixture-test assertion bug at
`test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/ecto_user_test.exs:30`:

```elixir
assert :registered in Keyword.get_values(attrs, :lane_schema_kind)
```

`Module.register_attribute(__MODULE__, :lane_schema_kind, persist: true)`
(without `accumulate: true`) serialises the attribute value into the
BEAM attributes chunk wrapped in a list. So `__info__(:attributes)`
returns `[..., lane_schema_kind: [:registered]]` — the value is itself
a list. `Keyword.get_values/2` then returns `[[:registered]]`, and
`:registered in [[:registered]]` is `false`. The test fails identically
before, during, and after any `:cover` operation.

## Evidence

`priv/helper_scripts/spike_19_repro.exs` (this commit) reproduces the
full lifecycle and prints PRE / POST `:cover.compile_beam` /
POST `:cover.stop` / POST full-reset snapshots of every fixture
invariant. Every snapshot reports `attrs[:lane_schema_kind] = [[:registered]]`
— independent of cover. Filtered output:

```
---- snapshot: PRE :cover.compile_beam ----
  __info__(:md5)           = 28D0D174FDB29D296DC0FE7DD5E55506
  attrs[:lane_schema_kind] = [[:registered]]
  __schema_kind__/0        = :registered
  name/0                   = :string
  age/0                    = :integer
  birthday/1 (30)          = 31

---- snapshot: POST :cover.compile_beam ----
  :code.which/1            = :cover_compiled
  __info__(:md5)           = 419BA350BBCD10873F9882AA41BFCFAE
  attrs[:lane_schema_kind] = [[:registered]]
  __schema_kind__/0        = :registered
  name/0                   = :string
  age/0                    = :integer
  birthday/1 (30)          = 31

---- snapshot: POST :cover.stop ----
  :code.which/1            = .../mutagen_spike_19_ebin/Elixir.LaneFixture.EctoUser.beam
  __info__(:md5)           = 28D0D174FDB29D296DC0FE7DD5E55506
  attrs[:lane_schema_kind] = [[:registered]]
  ... (all five other invariants restored byte-for-byte)
```

The MD5 round-trips post-stop. The `attrs[:lane_schema_kind]` value is
the same nested-list shape in every snapshot — it never depended on
cover.

`priv/helper_scripts/spike_19_attrs.exs` is a tiny standalone confirming
that `Keyword.get_values/2` on a `persist: true` attribute returns a
list-of-lists, independent of any `:cover` involvement.

## Why not Option A or C

- **Not Option A** (fold into `.29`): the root cause is not in
  `:cover.compile_beam` / coverage instrumentation; there is nothing
  for `.29`'s combined baseline+coverage instrumented run to fold in.
  Deferring would mask a known fix behind unrelated work.

- **Not Option C** (non-`:cover` parallel scenario): the `:cover` path
  is already proven byte-faithful by this spike's evidence. A parallel
  scenario would add test surface without addressing the actual
  fixture-test bug.

- **Option B** is the right routing: small, localised, and lands
  Spike-I un-skip evidence cleanly.

## Follow-up

Opened `mutagen-wrd.32` (the `.19b` follow-up requested by the spike
template) with the focused fix plan: change the persisted-attribute
assertion to `List.flatten/1` or `Keyword.fetch!`, un-skip Scenario 7
in `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs`, and confirm the
bytecode-identical-MD5 check at the end of the scenario stays green.

## Spec-side correction

`.spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md` lines 43 + 116 still carry the
"cover-instrumentation issue" framing this spike disproved. Updating
those is out of `.19`'s Allowed touches (`.spec/decisions/` is not on
the allow-list); the correction lives more naturally inside `.32`'s
un-skip commit, where the framing change pairs with the assertion fix.

## Merge gate

- [x] Investigation conclusion recorded as a ticket comment (this one)
- [x] README updated with root cause + deferral target (`mutagen-wrd.32`)
- [x] `.19b` opened (created as `mutagen-wrd.32` per bw sequential ID)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE (orchestrator-owned)

IMPLEMENTED

- priv/helper_scripts/spike_19_attrs.exs (minimal standalone confirming the persisted-attribute keyword-get_values shape)

Tests added: none (spike outcome is a decision + investigation
artefacts; the Scenario 7 un-skip + assertion fix lives in `mutagen-wrd.32`,
the `.19b` follow-up).

Verification stubs flipped: none. The spec stubs that gate Spike-I
(`mutagen.coverage` / `mutagen.mutation_pipeline`) un-skip together
when `mutagen-wrd.32` lands. `.19` is the decision node only.

Audit-expanded scope: none (iteration 1; nothing outside Allowed
touches).

Deferred must requirements: none. Every Merge-gate item the ticket
named is satisfied within Allowed touches:
- Investigation conclusion as a ticket comment — SPIKE DECISION above

- `.19b` opened: created as `mutagen-wrd.32` (bw uses sequential IDs)
- Auditor APPROVE is orchestrator-owned

Summary: 4-hour spike completed in well under budget with a clean
root cause. Scenario 7's baseline_red is not a `:cover` interaction —
it is a fixture-test assertion bug (`Keyword.get_values` on a
`persist: true` attribute returns a list-of-lists). Disposition is
Option B: `mutagen-wrd.32` opened with the focused fix plan (assertion
fix + un-skip + spec-side framing correction). README + CHANGELOG
reflect the corrected story; reproduction scripts are committed for
audit-trail traceability.

Project verification: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean,
`mix test` 362 tests / 0 failures / 13 excluded.

VERIFY: PASS

Mechanical checks:

- Files outside Allowed globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0 (none gated by this spike; .19 is a decision node — stubs for mutagen.coverage / mutagen.mutation_pipeline flip when .32 un-skips Scenario 7)
- Spike merge-gate items:
  - Decision (A/B/C) recorded as comment: yes — Option B at the 2026-05-15T00:48:04Z comment on mutagen-wrd.19
  - README updated with root cause + deferral target: yes — "Known limitations" entry 5 (README.md:158-173), corrected framing + pointer to mutagen-wrd.32
  - Follow-up ticket mutagen-wrd.32 opened (Option B): yes — concrete fix plan with named file/line (test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/ecto_user_test.exs:30), the persisted-attribute / Keyword.get_values root cause, two named assertion-fix shapes (List.flatten/1 or Keyword.fetch!), and un-skip target at test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs:789
- Helper-script repros faithful to the claim: yes
  - spike_19_repro.exs: ran; MD5 round-trips post :cover.stop (PRE=28D0D174... == POST_STOP=28D0D174...); attrs[:lane_schema_kind] = [[:registered]] in every snapshot (PRE / POST compile_beam / POST stop / POST reload / INSIDE cover before+after ExUnit / POST full reset). Shows the EctoUserTest failure happens INSIDE the cover-running ExUnit with right: [[:registered]] — identical to the cover-free assertion shape. Demonstrates: cover lifecycle is byte-faithful; failure mode is the assertion, not :cover.
  - spike_19_attrs.exs: ran; attrs = [vsn: [...], foo: [:bar]], Keyword.get_values(attrs, :foo) = [[:bar]], :bar in Keyword.get_values? = false, :bar in List.flatten(...)? = true. Standalone confirmation independent of :cover.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No lib/ touched. No :cover fix code. No e2e suite refactor. Scenario 7 remains @tag :skip (fix correctly routed to mutagen-wrd.32). Implementer correctly defers the .spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md framing correction to .32 (not on .19's Allowed list).
- Project verification: pass (mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test 362 tests / 0 failures / 13 excluded)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Spike completed well under the 4h cap with a clean, evidence-backed root cause that disproves the prior "cover-instrumentation issue" framing.
- The helper-script artefacts give .32 (and any future auditor) a reproducible evidence trail; running them today produces output that matches the comment's snapshot transcript.
- The spec-side stale framing in .spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md (lines ~42-44 and ~116) does carry the disproved "cover-instrumentation issue with Ecto-style DSLs" wording. .19's Allowed touches don't include .spec/decisions/, and the implementer flagged this — appropriate to land in .32's un-skip commit where assertion-fix + framing-correction pair naturally.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit, orchestrated)

title: DISCOVERY: :ecto_user_scenario @tag :skip — spike root cause, decide deferral or fix path
